content: ATC training-sim scenario authoring approaches: (1) hand-scripted per-aircraft entries (type, callsign, start altitude/speed/position, cleared flight level, route/flight-path) commonly in XML or a structured text/data format; (2) modular scenario design building a baseline traffic file first, then layering stressors (weather, runway change, frequency congestion, aircraft mix, emergencies) one at a time; (3) newer research tools moving toward graphical/node-based event-driven scenario editors, but manual scripted files remain the standard baseline approach in both commercial and hobby ATC sims.
